trade.summary: South Korea and Malaysia have solid trade ties under the ASEAN-Korea FTA. Malaysia is a key supplier of LNG and electronics components to Korea. Korea exports semiconductors, chemicals, and machinery to Malaysia. Samsung, SK Hynix, and LG have significant manufacturing or procurement relationships with Malaysian electronics firms.

diplomatic.summary: Korea-Malaysia relations are warm, with both countries cooperating through ASEAN+3 frameworks and bilateral mechanisms. Malaysia's 'Look East Policy' (modeled partly on Korea's development experience alongside Japan) created natural affinity. Korea and Malaysia cooperate on economic development, education, and technology transfer. Korea-Malaysia relations lack major political friction.

military.summary: Moderate defense relationship. Korea has sold military equipment to Malaysia, including FA-50 light combat aircraft (contracted). Both countries participate in multilateral maritime security exercises. No formal bilateral defense treaty.

financial.summary: Korean FDI in Malaysia spans electronics manufacturing, construction, and finance. Hyundai, Samsung, and LG have Malaysian operations. Malaysian sovereign wealth (Khazanah) has some Korean equity exposure. Bilateral investment has been growing.

energy.summary: Malaysia is a significant LNG supplier to South Korea. Petronas MLNG supplies Korean utilities under long-term contracts. This LNG relationship is strategically important for Korea's energy security. Korean energy companies have invested in Malaysian upstream oil and gas.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation in electronics, engineering, and environmental science. Korean universities have exchange programs with Malaysian institutions. KOICA runs technical cooperation programs. Collaboration in ICT and smart manufacturing is expanding.
